title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Finance, lets you connect your bank accounts
description: ChatGPT can now take care of your personal finances, which definitely sounds like a good idea. In the US, subscribers to ChatGPT Pro can now connect accounts from over 12,000 financial institutions, thanks to a partnership OpenAI has established with the financial connection service Plaid. Once you connect your account(s) to ChatGPT, you'll see a dashboard of your portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments. ChatGPT can answer questions about your spending or plans to save money with context from your actual financial situation. You can manage the...

title: Honor 600 review
Samsung Galaxy M47 runs Geekbench, reveals the chipset powering it
description: In March, the upcoming Samsung Galaxy M47 was spotted in the GSM Association's IMEI database, and this confirmed that the device was in development. Today, a prototype has been spotted in the Geekbench online database, and this has revealed the fact that it's powered by the Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 SoC. This was paired with 8GB of RAM in the prototype which ran the benchmark. More options could be offered as well. The phone will run Android 16 when it launches, with Samsung's One UI 8 on top. These are all the details we know about it at the moment, stay tuned for more leaks and rumors...

title: Tecno Pova 8 Pro certifications reveal its battery capacity, RAM and storage
description: The Tecno Pova 8 was spotted in the Google Play Console last month, so we already knew it was coming. Now the Pova 8 Pro has received a certification of its own, confirming that it will be joining the vanilla model in the company's roster. The Pova 8 Pro will of course be the successor to the Pova 7 Pro, which launched last summer. The Pova 8 Pro has been certified by TUV Rheinland and the FCC. These certifications have revealed that it has a battery with a rated capacity of 6,340 mAh, which will undoubtedly be marketed as a typical capacity of 6,500 mAh. The phone's outline can...

title: Oppo Find X9s price in India leaks again and it's higher this time
description: The Oppo Find X9s initially became official last month, and Oppo is launching it in India on May 21. Earlier this month, a rumor claimed it would start at INR 70,000 ($729). Today a new rumor says the Find X9s will actually start at INR 79,999 ($834) instead. That's a pretty wild difference, and obviously we don't know which rumor is accurate, so take all of this with a hefty pinch of salt still. The Oppo Find X9s comes with a 6.59-inch 1256x2760 AMOLED touchscreen with 120Hz refresh rate and 3,600-nit peak brightness, the Dimensity 9500s SoC at the helm, 12GB of LPDDR5X RAM, 256/512GB...

title: Samsung's Exynos 2700 SoC for Galaxy S27 series to reportedly ditch WLP technology
description: Samsung’s next-generation flagship chipset, the Exynos 2700, could adopt a new packaging design, according to a recent report. The company is reportedly planning to move away from Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging (FOWLP) technology for the upcoming SoC. Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging (FOWLP), which Samsung has used since the Exynos 2400, reportedly helped improve thermal performance. However, the technology is said to have been less profitable for the company due to its complex and costly manufacturing process. According to the report, which cites an industry official, Samsung plans to...

title: Kuo: Apple is testing iPhone chipset production at Intel
description: Earlier this month, we saw a report which claimed that Apple is looking to diversify its chip production away from TSMC with Intel and Samsung as potential partners. Noted Apple analyst Ming Chi-Kuo’s latest post also backs up these claims alongside a few more details. Apple and Intel have apparently kicked off legacy Apple silicon chip production on Intel’s 18A-P process, which is equivalent to TSMC’s process used on the A18 Pro chip. According to the report, these are low-end chips set to be used in iPhone, iPads and Macs, and Kuo estimates that 80% of the chips are meant for iPhone....

title: Verizon joins Project Glasswing to test Anthropic's Claude Mythos model on its infrastructure
description: Today Verizon is announcing that it's joining Anthropic's Project Glasswing to test the Claude Mythos Preview model. This model, according to Anthropic, is capable of identifying complex vulnerabilities in critical software and operating systems at unprecedented speed. Due to its specialized capabilities, Anthropic set up Project Glasswing as a way to grant access to the Mythos Preview model to "a select group of global security leaders dedicated to protecting essential infrastructure and committed to sharing findings and best practices across industries", Verizon's press release on the...

title: DJI reveals the Osmo Pocket 4P tailored for independent filmmakers
description: During this year's Cannes Film Festival, DJI took the stage for a brief moment and introduced the Osmo Pocket 4P, which is a more advanced version of the regular Osmo Pocket 4 that the company introduced last month. While the regular Pocket 4 is aimed toward mainstream content creators and vloggers, the Pocket 4P is tailored for professional, independent filmmakers. With its powerful, yet compact hardware, the Pocket 4P wants to replace a good chunk of the heavy equipment involved in filmmaking. Hence, the product was introduced, although not fully disclosed, at the Cannes Film...

title: Pair of upcoming Xbox controllers emerges in listings
description: Microsoft has two new Xbox controllers in the works and we now have images and their key specs. The leaked details shared by Tecnoblog showcase the Xbox Cloud Gaming and Elite Series 3 controllers. Both emerged in listings from Brazil’s Anatel certification agency. Xbox Cloud Gaming controller (image: Tecnoblog) The Cloud Gaming controller features a narrow shape that mimics some third-party options like the 8BitDo. It comes in black and white colors and features the standard set of controls, including two joysticks, a D-pad, XYAB buttons, bumpers and triggers. We can also spot...

title: Realme announces return to Finnish market on May 18 with three devices
description: Realme has announced its return to the Finnish market with an official post on its recently-activated Instagram and TikTok accounts. In a digital letter to Finnish users, Realme describes itself as a young tech brand that was founded in 2018, and that it's the world's fastest-growing smartphone brand. The letter also admits that Realme is a part of the Oppo smartphone family, which also includes OnePlus. Speaking of, reports suggest that OnePlus is shrinking its operation in Europe, which neatly suggests Realme as a possible replacement. In the same announcement post, Realme confirms...

title: These Android phones will soon receive AirDrop support in Quick Share
description: The ability to share files directly from Android devices to iPhones is gradually expanding to more smartphones. During The Android Show: I/O Edition, Google announced that Quick Share’s AirDrop interoperability will soon be available on devices from OnePlus, Oppo, and Honor. Google first introduced Quick Share-AirDrop interoperability with the Pixel 10 series last year before bringing it to older Pixel models, Samsung’s Galaxy S26 lineup, and more recently, the Oppo Find X9 Ultra and vivo X300 Ultra. As of right now, the feature is also supported on the Pixel 8a and the Oppo Find...

title: Android users can now share files with iPhones via QR code
description: Google is rolling out a new feature on Quick Share on Android that allows users to send files to iOS devices using a QR code. If Quick Share on your Android phone does not offer AirDrop compatibility yet, this new QR code-based method offers a simple alternative. Recipients can scan the code with their iPhone to quickly access and download the shared files. The new feature was announced during The Android Show: I/O Edition earlier this week. When an iPhone user scans the QR code with the Camera app, the selected files are securely uploaded to the cloud and delivered through a...

title: Honor Robot Phone's launch timeframe officially confirmed
description: Last year, we heard that Honor's Robot Phone would enter mass production in the first half of 2026. Later, rumors surfaced that the Robot Phone would launch in Q3 2026. Well, the rumors have proven true, as Honor has officially confirmed that the Robot Phone will launch in Q3 2026, meaning anytime between July and September. This confirmation came at Cannes China Night, where Honor showcased the Robot Phone to demonstrate its imaging capabilities to the guests. Additionally, Honor once again confirmed that the Robot Phone, which features a 4DoF gimbal system, will be the first to...

title: Ayaneo reveals when the Next 2 and Konkr Fit Windows handhelds will ship
description: Ayaneo unveiled the Next 2 Windows gaming handheld in December, and the company has now provided an update on its shipping schedule. According to Ayaneo, the Next 2 will start shipping in June to Indiegogo backers. Ayaneo's suppliers have already started production of the key components of the Next 2, including the motherboard, screen, battery, and "other core parts". Non-critical components have already been completed. The company expects to start trial production of the handheld in early June, and mass production in mid-June. Thus, shipping the first orders will take place in...

title: Honor Win Turbo is coming this month, teaser campaign officially kicks off
description: Honor launched the Win and Win RT smartphones back in December, kicking off the new series, and now the company is almost ready to introduce us to the third member of the family. Honor has started teasing the Win Turbo, which is coming soon. Unfortunately, no details about the device have been outed just yet, but Honor does promise it will be released later this month. The image above is all we have to go by right now, it shows the Honor Win Turbo's outline and, well, it's clear that this belongs to the Win family with the overextended camera island and Win branding in its bottom...

title: Google is bringing a long-awaited dialer update to Android
description: If you are constantly making VoIP calls on apps like WhatsApp, Telegram or Messenger from your Android phone, then you may soon be able to dial contacts on those apps straight from your default phone dialer app. Google has been testing this feature for a while now, and it seems like it is finally ready for a wider rollout under the Calling accounts domain. The folks over at Android Authority were able to get it running on a Pixel 9 using the latest Android Canary 2605 build. The Calling accounts feature was seen inside the settings for the Phone by Google app, which is the Pixel native...

title: vivo X500 series once again tipped to pack 7,000mAh batteries
description: The vivo X500 series is expected to debut later this year as the successor to the current X300 lineup. Although the official launch is several months away, leaks have already revealed key details about the upcoming phones. A new report has now once again hinted at the battery capacities of the lineup. According to tipster Smart Pikachu, every model in the upcoming vivo X500 series will feature a battery with a capacity of at least 7,000mAh. vivo X200 Pro and X300 Pro This claim is not entirely new, as a separate leak from November last year suggested the same. Still, the latest...

title: iQOO Z11 is headed to India, here's when to expect it and how much it will cost
description: The iQOO Z11 launched in China in March and then earlier this month a device with the same name (but slightly different specs) appeared in Malaysia. Today a tipster over on X claims the Malaysian Z11 is also headed to India. It will arrive next month, most likely on or around June 15. Like the Malaysian Z11, the Indian Z11 will be powered by the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 SoC, and it will run Android 16 out of the box with vivo's OriginOS 6 on top. The Z11 is said to be offered in India for under INR 30,000 ($313). The phone comes with a 6.83-inch 1260x2800 AMOLED screen with 144Hz refresh...

title: Samsung will be using the Dimensity 9500 chipset
description: A future Samsung device will be powered by MediaTek's Dimensity 9500 SoC, according to information from code inside Samsung's AI Core app. The code references the Dimensity 9500 by its model number. It doesn't tell us exactly which device this will be, but it stands to reason that we're talking about the upcoming Galaxy Tab S12 series, since the Tab S11 line used the Dimensity 9400+. Thus, this would be a normal and expected upgrade for the Korean company's tablet family. Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 Ultra The Galaxy Tab S11 devices were unveiled in early September, and that timeline...
